$comment: Claim -> test map enforced by scripts/check-claims.mjs (AGENTS.md rule 2). Each anchor must appear verbatim in its source file, and every listed test title must exist in tests/**/*.test.js. Every test titled 'PROMISE: ...' must be registered here. When you add a behavioral claim to README.md, docs/, AGENTS.md, or CHANGELOG.md, register it; when you soften or remove a claim, update or remove its entry.

claims.anchor: The scheduler starts ready tasks in deterministic graph order and runs independent tasks in parallel up to the configured concurrency.
tests: runJob schedules ready tasks in parallel up to concurrency
orders tasks deterministically with dependencies before dependents

claims.anchor: `--force` re-runs tasks while still recording fresh cache entries
tests: force re-runs cached tasks and refreshes the cache entry

claims.anchor: `--dry-run` prints the plan with predicted cache hits without executing
tests: planJob predicts cache hits without executing tasks

claims.anchor: `cache clear` resets the task cache, and `gc` prunes old run records and cache entries unused for `--cache-days` days
tests: cache clear and gc maintain local pipeline state

claims.anchor: `gc` prunes run records and cache entries unused for `--cache-days` days
tests: cache clear and gc maintain local pipeline state
cache hits refresh mtimes and gc prunes cold cache entries

claims.anchor: `gc` keeps the newest run records and prunes task-cache entries unused for `--cache-days` days.
tests: cache clear and gc maintain local pipeline state

claims.anchor: Cache hits refresh their last-used time, and `--cache-days 0` disables cache pruning.
tests: cache hits refresh mtimes and gc prunes cold cache entries

claims.anchor: Runs also auto-prune to the newest 50 records; set `ASYNC_PIPELINE_KEEP_RUNS` to change the limit or `0` to disable.
tests: run auto-prunes records, emits json, and finds config from subdirectories

claims.anchor: Task output buffers cap at 8 MiB per stream (`ASYNC_PIPELINE_MAX_LOG_BYTES`, `0` = unlimited); stored logs keep the tail.
tests: task logs are capped by ASYNC_PIPELINE_MAX_LOG_BYTES with a truncation marker

claims.anchor: The CLI finds `pipeline.ts` from any subdirectory by walking up.
tests: run auto-prunes records, emits json, and finds config from subdirectories

claims.anchor: Make cache behavior explicit through declared task inputs, cache refs, and task config.
tests: PROMISE: per-task inputs isolate cache invalidation
glob inputs are resolved deterministically and included in cache keys
rejects unknown cache stores and policies

claims.anchor: Read pipeline metadata without cloning sources, running `prepare`, executing tasks, or evaluating deferred shell callbacks.
tests: CLI exposes metadata, matrix, source list, source sync, and namespaced run-task
normalizes sources and allows declared external task refs as metadata

claims.anchor: Generate and check a pinned GitHub Actions workflow from `pipeline.ts`.
tests: github generate writes a current workflow and lock
renders github workflow triggers and bootloader steps

claims.anchor: The checked-in workflow targets Node `>= 24` on GitHub-hosted Linux (`ubuntu-latest`) and macOS (`macos-latest`) runners
tests: renders github job runner labels and runner matrices

claims.anchor: Use `job({ github: { runsOn } })` to select a hosted runner or self-hosted label set, and `job({ github: { runsOnMatrix } })` to fan a job out across multiple runner label sets
tests: renders github job runner labels and runner matrices
github runner config rejects invalid and conflicting settings

claims.anchor: `sandbox.container(...)` declares OCI-compatible container image intent; OCI is the standard container image format used by Docker, Apple container, Podman, containerd, and registries.
tests: normalizes container sandboxes and execution profiles
container sandboxes resolve to selected providers without leaking undeclared env

claims.anchor: Use `--execution <id>` to select a profile, or `--sandbox <id> --provider docker|apple-container|lima` to choose a provider for `sandbox.container(...)`.
tests: runPipelineCli validates execution profiles before command-policy mock
container sandboxes resolve to selected providers without leaking undeclared env

claims.anchor: Isolation is opt-in: select it with `--sandbox`, `--execution`, or programmatic run options; `sandbox.container(...)` is portable OCI image intent
tests: runPipelineCli validates execution profiles before command-policy mock
normalizes container sandboxes and execution profiles
container sandboxes resolve to selected providers without leaking undeclared env

claims.anchor: `job({ execution: "..." })` selects a profile for local CLI defaults and generated GitHub bootloaders.
tests: normalizes container sandboxes and execution profiles
renders github execution profiles as runner defaults and CLI execution args

claims.anchor: `execution.github(...)` supplies GitHub runner defaults and generated workflows pass `--execution <id>` to the pipeline command.
tests: renders github execution profiles as runner defaults and CLI execution args
execution profiles reject unknown sandboxes and unsupported apple container runners

claims.anchor: If a generated command exists but is not claimed by the lock, it fails with `ASYNC_PIPELINE_SYNC_CONFLICT`.
tests: resolves package-name targets and rejects unmanaged conflicts
detects stale synced task manifests and locks

claims.anchor: generation fails with `ASYNC_PIPELINE_SYNC_CONFLICT` instead of overwriting your work
tests: resolves package-name targets and rejects unmanaged conflicts

claims.anchor: a hand-edited workflow or script fails CI with a diffable reason
tests: detects stale synced task manifests and locks
sync CLI generates and checks configured targets
github generate writes a current workflow and lock

claims.anchor: `prepare` runs inside that source checkout before source tasks run.
tests: runs a path source task with root-owned dynamic prepare and cache reuse

claims.anchor: Run many-repo impact checks with explicit dependent repos and namespaced task refs such as `storefront:test`.
tests: composes source pipeline tasks into a namespaced graph

claims.anchor: Nested task groups flatten with `.`: `claims.default` runs as `claims`, and `claims.report` runs as `claims.report`.
tests: task groups flatten default paths and resolve group-local dependencies
run-task dry-run accepts local and source task group ids

claims.anchor: Source namespaces still use `:`, so `storefront:claims.report` means task `claims.report` from source `storefront`.
tests: task groups keep source namespace parsing unchanged
run-task dry-run accepts local and source task group ids

claims.anchor: "pipeline:task:claims.report": "async-pipeline run-task claims.report"
tests: renders opt-in raw task scripts for flattened task group ids

claims.anchor: Unknown fields in the pipeline, tasks, taskDefaults, jobs, or a job's `github` config are rejected with `ASYNC_PIPELINE_UNKNOWN_FIELD`
tests: rejects unknown config fields with the field name

claims.anchor: Declaration helpers attach non-enumerable metadata under `Symbol.for("@async/pipeline.declaration")`, so JSON output and enumerable config shape stay unchanged.
tests: declaration factories expose non-enumerable metadata without changing JSON output

claims.anchor: The declaration brand is a discriminator, not trust: branded task, shell, and agent nodes are still validated, and unknown fields are still rejected.
tests: portable branded task and shell nodes normalize through validation

claims.anchor: Optional section factories such as `tasks({ ... })`, `jobs({ ... })`, and `sources({ ... })` are accepted without double wrapping; plain top-level section objects remain the default authoring style.
tests: explicit section factories are accepted and mismatched sections are rejected

claims.anchor: Nested task groups flatten with `.`. A child named `default` is the group default, so `tasks.claims.default` normalizes to task id `claims`, while `tasks.claims.report` normalizes to `claims.report`. `index` remains accepted as a compatibility alias for older task groups.
tests: task groups flatten default paths and resolve group-local dependencies

claims.anchor: Within a task group, a dependency like `dependsOn: ["report"]` resolves to the group-local task `claims.report` when that task exists; source refs such as `storefront:claims.report` keep using `:` for the source namespace.
tests: task groups flatten default paths and resolve group-local dependencies
task groups keep source namespace parsing unchanged

claims.anchor: Raw task objects still work, but branded `task({})` removes the empty-task ambiguity inside a group.
tests: task groups flatten default paths and resolve group-local dependencies

claims.anchor: Raw task sync is opt-in and generates names like `pipeline:task:typecheck` and `pipeline:task:claims.report`.
tests: renders opt-in raw task scripts for flattened task group ids

claims.anchor: "pipeline:task:claims.report": "async-pipeline run-task claims.report"
tests: renders opt-in raw task scripts for flattened task group ids

claims.anchor: On `workflow_dispatch` only jobs with a `manual` trigger run implicitly
tests: matches github jobs from event context

claims.anchor: `run` and `run-task` hold `.async/run.lock` for the duration of a run.
tests: concurrent runs in the same project fail fast with ASYNC_PIPELINE_RUN_ACTIVE
run locks reject active holders and reclaim stale locks

claims.anchor: A lock whose holder process is dead is reclaimed automatically
tests: run locks reject active holders and reclaim stale locks

claims.anchor: Execution records (`.async/runs/<run-id>/execution.json`) and stored cache results carry `schemaVersion` (currently `1`), and records include the owning `pid` so `doctor` can tell a crashed run from a live one.
tests: execution records and cache entries carry schemaVersion 1
doctor warns about stale running run records

claims.anchor: | `130` | Interrupted by SIGINT (Ctrl-C); tasks were terminated and the execution record finalized. |
tests: PROMISE: interrupting the CLI terminates running task processes

claims.anchor: | `141` | CLI output pipe closed (EPIPE, e.g. piping into `head`); tasks were terminated and the record finalized. |
tests: PROMISE: a closed output pipe terminates tasks and finalizes the run

claims.anchor: | `143` | Terminated by SIGTERM; same shutdown path as SIGINT. |
tests: CLI exits 143 and finalizes records after SIGTERM

claims.anchor: Task-level timeouts surface as command exit code `124` inside the task result; the run itself exits `1`.
tests: task timeout fails the execution and records the error

claims.anchor: | `ASYNC_PIPELINE_KEEP_RUNS` | Run-record auto-prune limit applied after each run (default `50`, `0` disables). |
tests: run auto-prunes records, emits json, and finds config from subdirectories

claims.anchor: | `ASYNC_PIPELINE_MAX_LOG_BYTES` | Per-stream task output buffer cap in bytes (default 8 MiB, `0` = unlimited, minimum `4096`). |
tests: task logs are capped by ASYNC_PIPELINE_MAX_LOG_BYTES with a truncation marker

claims.anchor: | `ASYNC_PIPELINE_ENVIRONMENT` | Environment name checked by `command.requireEnvironment(...)`. |
tests: requireEnvironment command policy denies unless ASYNC_PIPELINE_ENVIRONMENT matches

claims.anchor: | `CI` | When set, runs record `mode: "ci"`. |
tests: CI env records ci execution mode

claims.anchor: A task's cache key depends on the content of its own declared inputs, its dependencies' cache keys, and its resolved steps
tests: PROMISE: per-task inputs isolate cache invalidation
dependency cache keys invalidate direct dependents
cache keys omit candidate fingerprints and absolute working directories

claims.anchor: A second run of an unchanged pipeline is fully `cached`.
tests: PROMISE: a second run of an unchanged pipeline is fully cached

claims.anchor: A cache hit restores declared outputs, or downgrades to a recorded miss when it cannot.
tests: file cache restores declared outputs on cache hit
result-only file cache entries for output tasks rerun and repopulate outputs

claims.anchor: `.git/`, `.async/`, and `node_modules/` are pruned from input walks at any depth.
tests: default pruning skips .git, .async, and node_modules at any depth

claims.anchor: Resolved secret values never appear in echoed output or stored run logs.
tests: resolved secret values are redacted from stored task logs
PROMISE: the GitHub token never appears in publish output

claims.anchor: never on unrelated tasks' inputs, absolute machine paths, or the task's own outputs
tests: PROMISE: declared outputs do not feed back into a task's own cache inputs
cache inputs ignore local state directories and declared outputs

claims.anchor: interrupting the CLI never orphans task processes
tests: PROMISE: interrupting the CLI terminates running task processes

claims.anchor: when CLI output is piped to a closed reader (EPIPE)
tests: PROMISE: a closed output pipe terminates tasks and finalizes the run

claims.anchor: Stream CLI progress (plan and per-task status) while a run is executing
tests: PROMISE: the CLI streams the plan before task output and prefixes task lines

claims.anchor: Fail fast with `ASYNC_PIPELINE_INPUT_CYCLE` on `namedInputs` cycles
tests: named input cycles fail fast at definePipeline time

claims.anchor: Kill the whole process tree on task timeout
tests: task timeout fails the execution and records the error

claims.anchor: Docker sandboxes now forward only pipeline-defined env
tests: docker commands forward only allowlisted env keys

claims.anchor: Container sandbox abstraction: `sandbox.container(...)` now declares portable OCI image intent, execution profiles select local or GitHub providers
tests: normalizes container sandboxes and execution profiles
renders github execution profiles as runner defaults and CLI execution args
container sandboxes resolve to selected providers without leaking undeclared env

claims.anchor: `command.requireEnvironment(...)` is enforced
tests: requireEnvironment command policy denies unless ASYNC_PIPELINE_ENVIRONMENT matches

claims.anchor: Write execution records, cache results, output manifests, and logs atomically
tests: writeFileAtomic publishes complete files and leaves no temp files behind

claims.anchor: Serialize local runs with `.async/run.lock`
tests: run locks reject active holders and reclaim stale locks

claims.anchor: Record `schemaVersion` and owner `pid` on execution records
tests: run records include schema version and owner pid

claims.anchor: Refresh task-cache mtimes on cache hits and let `gc --cache-days <n>` prune cold entries.
tests: cache hits refresh mtimes and gc prunes cold cache entries
cache clear and gc maintain local pipeline state

claims.anchor: Have `doctor` warn about unreadable run directories and stale `"running"` records.
tests: doctor warns about stale running run records

claims.anchor: Never leave an execution record in `"running"`
tests: a throwing deferred step factory fails the task and finalizes the record
PROMISE: a closed output pipe terminates tasks and finalizes the run

claims.anchor: missing variables, or unmapped variable values fail before shell commands run
tests: job env secrets fail before execution when missing
mapped env vars fail before execution when unmapped

claims.anchor: Stable releases publish to GitHub Packages as `@async/pipeline` before npm, so a stable version exists on the fallback registry even when npm publishing has an issue.
tests: PROMISE: stable releases mirror to GitHub Packages as @async/pipeline with the latest tag
PROMISE: the release chain publishes GitHub Packages before npm, and previews/snapshots are wired to their triggers

claims.anchor: Pushes to `main` that pass the verify chain publish an immutable `0.0.0-main.sha.<sha>` snapshot to GitHub Packages and move the `main` dist-tag.
tests: main snapshots publish 0.0.0-main.sha.<sha> and move the main tag while current
PROMISE: a superseded main snapshot never drags the main tag backwards
PROMISE: the release chain publishes GitHub Packages before npm, and previews/snapshots are wired to their triggers

claims.anchor: Same-repo pull requests publish an immutable `0.0.0-pr.<n>.sha.<sha>` preview and move the `pr-<number>` dist-tag; fork pull requests never publish previews.
tests: same-repo PRs publish an immutable preview version, move the pr tag, and upsert one comment
PROMISE: fork pull requests never publish preview packages

claims.anchor: Republishing an existing version skips cleanly instead of failing, so re-dispatched publish jobs stay green.
tests: PROMISE: republishing an existing version skips publish but still moves the dist-tag

claims.anchor: Unknown permission fields fail with `ASYNC_PIPELINE_UNKNOWN_FIELD`.
tests: rejects unknown github permission fields

claims.anchor: the generator restates `contents: read` automatically whenever a job grants anything else
tests: renders github job packages, issues, and pull-requests permissions with a contents fallback

claims.anchor: refuses to treat registry availability failures as a missing version
tests: PROMISE: a registry outage during the existence check fails loudly instead of publishing blind

claims.anchor: enforces release tag/package.json version parity on `release` events
tests: release events with a tag that does not match package.json fail before publishing
release events with a matching v-prefixed tag publish normally

claims.anchor: all exercised by this repo's own `release:check`
tests: basic-node-package: verify runs, re-runs cached, and sync artifacts are current
many-repo-impact-run: impact job runs both source repos and matrix plans them

claims.anchor: The wrapper now calls the exported `runCliMain()` explicitly, the guard realpath-resolves `argv[1]` so symlinked bin paths count as direct execution, and a regression test drives the public bin both directly and through a symlink.
tests: public package bin runs the CLI (direct and through a symlinked bin path)

claims.anchor: Failed `run`, `run-task`, and `github run` invocations now print each failed task's recorded error to stderr next to the final status line
tests: run prints failed task reasons to stderr next to the final status

claims.anchor: Agent cache keys include the resolved profile id, model, and prompt — never the adapter's command path.
tests: PROMISE: agent cache keys ignore the adapter binary path and change with model or prompt

claims.anchor: A cached agent task replays its declared outputs without invoking the adapter.
tests: PROMISE: a cached agent task replays declared outputs without re-invoking the adapter

claims.anchor: A request/response transcript is written to `.async/runs/<run-id>/agents/<task>.jsonl`. Transcripts and task logs redact resolved secret values.
tests: PROMISE: an agent step runs the selected profile and records a redacted transcript under the run's agents directory

claims.anchor: both `use` and `model` accept `env.var(...)`, resolved at run time from the task env
tests: PROMISE: env.var(...) selects the agent profile and model at run time

claims.anchor: keys include the resolved profile id, model, and prompt — never the adapter's command path — so a cached agent task replays declared outputs without invoking the adapter
tests: PROMISE: agent cache keys ignore the adapter binary path and change with model or prompt
PROMISE: a cached agent task replays declared outputs without re-invoking the adapter

claims.anchor: On task failure the runner writes a context pack to `.async/runs/<run-id>/context/<task>.json`: the error, a redacted log tail, the reproduction command, and the input diff against the task's last passing cache entry — content digests only, never file contents.
tests: PROMISE: failed tasks write a context pack with a redacted log tail and an input diff against the last passing run

claims.anchor: A task that has never passed reports `baselineMissing` instead of a diff.
tests: PROMISE: a task that has never passed reports a missing baseline instead of a diff

claims.anchor: every cache entry persists a per-file digest manifest (`inputs.json`) for the input state that produced it
tests: PROMISE: cache entries persist per-file input digests for the entry's input state

claims.anchor: `explain <task> --diff-inputs` reports the files that changed since the task last passed
tests: PROMISE: explain --diff-inputs reports the files that changed since a task last passed

claims.anchor: The MCP server is read-only by default: `run_job` is exposed only when the server is started with `--allow-run`.
tests: PROMISE: the MCP server answers initialize and exposes read-only inspection tools without run_job by default

claims.anchor: `run_job` acquires the same run lock, writes the same records, and replays the same cache as a CLI run
tests: PROMISE: MCP run_job with --allow-run runs the same scheduler and records run evidence

claims.anchor: `stdoutTo` lands the adapter's stdout as a task artifact after a successful step
tests: PROMISE: agent stdoutTo lands the adapter's stdout as a task artifact and caches it like any output

claims.anchor: `doctor` warns when an agent task declares no outputs
tests: PROMISE: doctor warns when an agent task declares no outputs

claims.anchor: mock adapter profile emitting an applicable unified diff with one line of context per hunk, full circle exercised by the examples suite
tests: agent-claims-repair: verify is green, the stale fixture fails, and the mock agent proposes an applicable patch
